rulings.Case Title: HUSTON JACKSON VS. GETHSEMANE BAPTIST CHURCH A NON-PROFIT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief From Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 21, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF HUSTON JACKSON Motion For Relief From Dismissal. Plaintiff's motion for relief from dismissal is denied. First, on 12/22/14, the court continued this motion and ordered Plaintiff to provide a courtesy copy of the moving papers in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.6B. The court warned Plaintiff that his failure to provide the documents would result in the motion being denied. Plaintiff failed to provide the papers. Second, the dismissal was entered over a year ago and the motion is untimely. CCP 473(b) requires that a motion for relief "shall be made within a reasonable time, in no case exceeding six months, after the judgment, dismissal, order, or proceeding was taken." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: HUSTON JACKSON VS. GETHSEMANE BAPTIST CHURCH A NON-PROFIT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Enforcement Of Terms Of Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 21, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF HUSTON JACKSON Motion For Enforcement Of Terms Of Settlement. Plaintiff's motion to enforce the settlement is denied. Plaintiff admits that Defendant never signed the agreement. CCP 664.6 requires that all parties sign the settlement to be enforceable under that provision. See Sully-Miller Contracting Co. v. Gledson/Cashman Construction, Inc. (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 30, 37. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL G. SCHINNER VS. RAYMOND ZHANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 21, 2015, Line 12, DEFENDANT HERSHORN & HENRY, LLP, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. The litigation privilege does not bar this action because plaintiff is complaining about defendant's conduct, not its publications or communications. See Rusheen v. Cohen (2006) 37 Cal.4th 1048, 1057, 1065. The court grants summary adjudication on the breach of contract cause of action. Even if the court assumes that both plaintiff and defendant H&H were third party beneficiaries under the Chicago Title/Zhang settlement agreement, there is no authority holding that plaintiff can sue defendant H&H for breach of contract. The parties to the underlying contract (Chicago Title and the Zhangs) were the promisors and obliged to perform the contract. The court denies summary adjudication on the intentional interference with contract cause of action. There are triable issues of material fact regarding defendant H&H's knowledge of the agreement, its conduct to cause the breach, and causation. The court denies summary adjudication on the conversion cause of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding plaintiff's ownership interest in the money. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: VASU D. ARORA VS. ARNOLD LAUB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Requesting Reconsideration To Modify, Amend Or Revoke Previous Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 21, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF VASU ARORA Motion To Requesting Reconsideration To Modify, Amend Or Revoke Previous Order. The motion for reconsideration is denied. The motion is untimely under CCP 1008 and the court lacks the jurisdiction to entertain the motion because judgment was entered on July 29, 2014. See Ramon v. Aerospace Corp. (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 1233, 1236. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: INSTITUTO LABORAL DE LA RAZA VS. CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC, DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 22, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC, DBA GREAT WESTERN COLLECTION BUREAU'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Dennis Hyde, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to February 11, 2015 to review compliance with the Court's order filed December 17, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to hydelaw@pacbell.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA STATE LANDS COMMISSION VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Appliation For Leave To File Amicus Curiae Brief And Amicus Uriae Brief In Support Of City And County Of San Francisco'S Demurrer To First Amended Petition For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate And Complaint For Declaratory And Injunctive Relief
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 21, 2015, Line 9, OTHER JOHN BURTON, SIERRA CLUB Appliation For Leave To File Amicus Curiae Brief And Amicus Uriae Brief In Support Of City And County Of San Francisco's Demurrer To First Amended Petition For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate And Complaint For Declaratory And Injunctive Relief. The matter is continued to February 19, 2015, per ex parte order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: GLOBAL STAFFING SERVICES, LLC VS. JACKNYFE, INC D/B/A/ ECHO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 21, 2015, Line 10, PLAINTIFF GLOBAL STAFFING SERVICES, LLC APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. The demurrer is continued to February 10, 2015, to give the moving party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of the demurrer with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. The court has the other papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 21, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT JACKNYFE, INC D/B/A/ ECHO, KHRISTOPHER LOUX, CHRIS SAAD, LEV WALKIN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is continued to February 10, 2015, to give the moving party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of the demurrer with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. The court has the other papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: RS ALAMEDA, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. GLUMAC, A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Glumac Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 21, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT GLUMAC, Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay. Grant, no opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)
